note: G# major is enharmonically equivalent to Ab major. Ab major (4 flats) is more commonly used in practice than G# major (8 sharps). This database uses G# notation but musicians typically prefer Ab major for readability.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Contains all chord tones plus safe extensions. Note: Ab major pentatonic (Ab, Bb, C, Eb, F) is more commonly notated.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Target the root and major 3rd for resolution. In practice, think Ab pentatonic for easier reading.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: The go-to scale for G#/Ab major. Perfect for pop, R&B, and contemporary music. Extremely common in Ab major context.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: The fundamental major scale. Note: Ab Ionian (Ab, Bb, C, Db, Eb, F, G) with 4 flats is preferred over G# with 8 sharps.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: All notes work over I major. In practice, use Ab major notation for clarity.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Complete freedom over G#/Ab major. Essential for full diatonic expression.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Raises the 4th creating dreamy quality. Ab Lydian (Ab, Bb, C, D, Eb, F, G) is more practical notation.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Emphasize the #11 for signature Lydian color. Think Ab Lydian in practice.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for Abmaj7#11 or modern I major sounds. Perfect for film scores and jazz.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Creates dominant 7th character. Ab Mixolydian is standard in practice.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: The b7 creates bluesy edge. Use Ab7 notation in practice.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for Ab7 chords and R&B progressions.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Contains all four chord tones. Use Abmaj7 notation in practice.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Emphasize the maj7. Think Ab Ionian for easier reading.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Complete freedom over Abmaj7. Perfect for jazz, pop, R&B.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: THE scale for Imaj7 in modern jazz. Use Ab Lydian in practice.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: The #11 is signature sound. Think Abmaj7#11.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: The definitive modern Imaj7 scale. Essential for jazz and R&B.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: The natural ii mode. Use Bb Dorian in practice for easier reading.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Emphasize the 13th for Dorian character. Think Bbm7 in practice.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: The definitive ii chord scale. Use Bb Dorian notation.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Contains all four chord tones. Bbm7 is standard notation.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: 13th crucial for Dorian color. Think Bbm7-Eb7-Abmaj7 in practice.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential for iim7. Use Bb Dorian notation for clarity.

scaleCompatibility.relationship: Natural iii mode with exotic b2
scaleCompatibility.rationale: The iii mode. Use C Phrygian in practice - much easier to read.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Emphasize the b2 for Phrygian color. Think Cm in Ab major.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for exotic iii minor. Cm notation is standard.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: The natural IV scale. Db Major is standard notation in practice.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Emphasize root and major 3rd. Use Db major for easier reading.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential for I-IV-V. Think Ab-Db-Eb progression.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Modern IV sound. Db Lydian is practical notation.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: The #11 creates bright quality. Think Dbmaj7#11.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for sophisticated IV. Db Lydian in practice.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Creates dominant 7th character. Eb Mixolydian is standard.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: The b7 creates dominant pull. Think Eb7 resolving to Ab.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential for V7. Use Eb7 notation in practice.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Contains all four chord tones. Eb7 is standard notation.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Emphasize 3rd and b7 tritone. Think Bbm7-Eb7-Abmaj7.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential for V7. Always use Eb7 notation in practice.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: The vi of G# major. Fm is standard notation - much more readable.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: All notes work perfectly. Think Ab-Eb-Fm-Db progression.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential for vi chords. Always use Fm in practice.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Contains all four chord tones. Fm7 is universally used.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: All notes work beautifully. Think Ab-Eb-Fm7-Db.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential for vim7. Use Fm7 notation always.

commonProgressions.name: I-IV-V (as Ab-Db-Eb)
commonProgressions.description: The foundation progression. Note: Always notated as Ab-Db-Eb in practice, not G#-C#-D#.
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Strong, soulful, and warm. Ab major is common in R&B and soul music.
famousSongs: Many R&B and soul classics use Ab-Db-Eb
commonProgressions.tips: ALWAYS write this as Ab-Db-Eb. G# major notation is impractical with 8 sharps. Ab major with 4 flats is standard.

commonProgressions.name: I-V-vi-IV (as Ab-Eb-Fm-Db)
commonProgressions.description: Modern pop progression. Written as Ab-Eb-Fm-Db in practice.
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Emotionally rich and soulful. Very common in contemporary R&B.
famousSongs: Someone Like You, countless pop ballads
commonProgressions.tips: Use Ab-Eb-Fm-Db notation. This is one of the most common progressions in Ab major.

commonProgressions.name: I-vi-IV-V (as Ab-Fm-Db-Eb)
famousSongs: Stand By Me, Blue Moon (when in Ab)

commonProgressions.name: ii-V-I (as Bbm7-Eb7-Abmaj7)
commonProgressions.description: Jazz standard progression. Written Bbm7-Eb7-Abmaj7 in practice.
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Sophisticated and smooth. Very common in jazz and R&B.
famousSongs: Autumn Leaves (when in Ab), jazz standards
commonProgressions.tips: ALWAYS write Bbm7-Eb7-Abmaj7. This is extremely common in jazz in Ab major.

commonProgressions.name: I-IV-vi-V (as Ab-Db-Fm-Eb)
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Warm and uplifting with emotional depth.
famousSongs: Common in soul and gospel music
commonProgressions.tips: Use Ab-Db-Fm-Eb notation. Common in R&B and soul.

commonProgressions.name: I-vi-ii-V (as Abmaj7-Fm7-Bbm7-Eb7)
famousSongs: I Got Rhythm variations in Ab

commonProgressions.name: I-bVII-IV-I (as Ab-Gb-Db-Ab)
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Bold and soulful with modal flavor.

practicalNote: IMPORTANT: G# major is ALWAYS written as Ab major in practice. Ab major (4 flats: Bb, Eb, Ab, Db) is infinitely more readable than G# major (8 sharps including double sharps). This database uses G# notation for systematic completeness, but musicians should ALWAYS think and write in Ab major. The enharmonic equivalents are provided for every chord and progression.
